title: Na'vi Adjectives
objective: Learn Na'vi words for describing things.

data.text: Time to move on to adjectives! Let's start by learning some new words

data.description: This word is used to describe the smell of living animals, as found around a watering hole or animal nest.
This word means 'on the opposite side'
This world means physically healthy. Notice the similarities with fpomronga'.

data.text: Adjectives in Na'vi are flexible, meaning they are able to come before or after the word they modify.
Adjectives are linked to the noun they modify by affixing the syllable a to the adjective, on the side closest to the noun. Let's see it in action!

data.question: Which is grammatically correct' Note that payoang means fish?
What is wrong with this sentence: payoanga fpomtokxnga

options.text: the a is at the end of the noun.
there's no a at the start of the noun
the adjective should be before the noun

data.text: 🎉 Great work! You now know how use adjectives!
